anti_patterns.name: string_concatenation_loop_java
anti_patterns.description: String concatenation with + in a loop creates a new String object each iteration. Use StringBuilder.
anti_patterns.fix: Use StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder(); sb.append(...); in the loop.

anti_patterns.name: raw_types
anti_patterns.description: Using raw types (List instead of List<String>) bypasses generics and loses compile-time type checking.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect raw Collection types without type parameters.
anti_patterns.fix: Always parameterize collections: List<String>, Map<Integer, User>, etc.

anti_patterns.name: catching_throwable
anti_patterns.description: Catching Throwable or Error catches JVM-level problems like OutOfMemoryError. Only catch Exception or specific subtypes.
anti_patterns.fix: Catch Exception or specific exception types. Never catch Throwable or Error.

anti_patterns.name: system_exit_in_library
anti_patterns.description: System.exit() should never be called in library code. It kills the entire JVM. Throw an exception instead.
anti_patterns.fix: Throw an appropriate exception. Let the top-level caller decide whether to exit.
